quiz.title: Panabo City Local History & Culture Quiz

questions.question: The name 'Panabo' originated from 'pana-sa-boboy'. What did this phrase refer to?

options.B: The bow and arrow used by early Aeta hunters
options.C: A native way of cooking wild boar
options.D: The seasonal flowering of forest canopy trees

questions.context_note: Early nomadic Aeta inhabitants used the 'pana-sa-boboy' (bow and arrow) to hunt wild animals for food in the lowlands.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Before Christian settlers arrived, what was the bustling trading settlement in Panabo called?
questions.context_note: At the beginning of the 20th century, arriving settlers found a thriving local marketplace and trading center known as Taboan.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: In what year did Panabo officially become an independent municipality?
questions.context_note: Panabo gained administrative independence from its mother municipality by presidential decree on July 19, 1949.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: Before becoming an independent municipality, Panabo was a component barangay of which town?
questions.context_note: Panabo was initially a territorial barangay administered under the jurisdiction of the municipality of Tagum (formerly known as Magugpo).
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Under whose presidential administration did Panabo officially achieve component cityhood status?
questions.context_note: President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo signed Republic Act No. 9015 on February 28, 2001, clear-pathing the way for cityhood.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: What historic Republic Act converted the municipality of Panabo into a component city?
questions.context_note: Republic Act No. 9015 is the official charter legislative document that authorized the transition of Panabo into a component city.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: What is the exact date of ratification and official foundation of 'Araw ng Panabo'?
questions.context_note: On March 31, 2001, local residents overwhelmingly ratified the cityhood law in a public plebiscite, establishing 'Araw ng Panabo'.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: Which major highway corridor directly bisects Panabo, cementing its position as a regional trade gateway?
questions.context_note: Panabo sits squarely along the Pan-Philippine Highway, making it a natural logistics hub between Davao City and Tagum City.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: What structural shift caused early indigenous Aetas to migrate further up into the mountain hinterlands?

options.A: Severe flooding from the Davao Gulf
options.B: The influx of Christian agricultural settlers from Visayas and Luzon
options.C: An executive decree by provincial leaders

questions.context_note: As waves of agricultural migrants arrived and cleared coastal trade centers in the early 1900s, Aetas moved inland to preserve their heritage.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What class of component city is Panabo categorized under in terms of regular financial income status?
questions.context_note: Panabo City is officially designated as a 3rd class component city within the province of Davao del Norte.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Aside from Aetas, which other groups co-existed in early Panabo?
questions.context_note: Early records note that Kalagans (Davao Muslims) and Animist Native Groups (ANGs) co-existed with the Aetas.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What globally prominent title is widely given to Panabo City due to its primary export engine?
questions.context_note: Panabo earned the definitive title 'Banana Capital of the Philippines' because of its extensive networks of export-grade Cavendish fields.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Which corporate enterprise operates the largest contiguous banana plantation in the world within Panabo?
questions.context_note: TADECO is headquartered and primarily located across Panabo, running a globally unparalleled agro-industrial complex.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: In what year was the Tagum Agricultural Development Corporation (TADECO) founded by Don Antonio Floirendo Sr.?
questions.context_note: TADECO was established on December 20, 1950, which fundamentally shifted Panabo's economic landscape toward massive industrial growth.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: Approximately how many hectares of banana fields does TADECO occupy for worldwide export production?
questions.context_note: TADECO spans roughly 6,900 hectares of high-yield agricultural terrain, packaging millions of boxes of fruit annually.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Which specific variety of banana is primarily grown in Panabo for global international markets?
questions.context_note: The sweet, robust Cavendish banana is the exclusive primary cultivar grown for maritime shipping and international distribution.
questions.source_title: Panabo Travel Guide: Best Things to Do & Top Attractions

questions.question: Apart from banana cultivation, what major aquaculture initiative handles maritime productivity in Panabo?
questions.context_note: The Panabo Mariculture Park hosts large-scale sea cages for sustainable commercial fish and shrimp production.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What crop was widely cultivated in Panabo BEFORE bananas became dominant?
questions.context_note: Abaca (Manila hemp) plantations dominated the local agricultural landscape prior to the late 1950s banana expansion.
questions.source_title: Panabo City Industrial History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Which major body of water shapes the coastal aquaculture bounds of Panabo?
questions.context_note: Panabo faces the nutrient-rich waters of the Davao Gulf, powering its local fishing and mariculture industries.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: What environmental factor makes Panabo's geography ideal for continuous agricultural harvesting?

options.B: A tropical climate with relatively stable, evenly distributed year-round rainfall
options.C: A prolonged six-month dry freezing window

questions.context_note: The lack of a distinct destructive dry season coupled with stable seasonal rainfall helps keep soil fertility high.
questions.source_title: About Panabo City - Discover Davao

questions.question: How many total component barangays make up the administrative territory of Panabo City?
questions.context_note: Panabo City is politically sub-divided into exactly 40 distinct urban and rural barangays.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Which of the following is an interior agricultural barangay named after a globally iconic chocolate crop?
questions.context_note: Barangay Cacao is a thriving agricultural locality within the city named directly after the cacao tree.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Which highly populated urban barangay houses the main commercial centers and historical business spaces?
questions.context_note: Barangay Gredu is historically one of the most populous, heavily commercialized center points of the city.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: What is the primary, most widely spoken native tongue used by a majority of Panaboans?
questions.context_note: Due to extensive migrations from the central islands, Cebuano/Bisaya serves as the city's common lingua franca.
questions.source_title: About Panabo City - Discover Davao

questions.question: What approximate land area does Panabo City cover along its fertile plains?
questions.context_note: The city covers 251.23 square kilometers, taking up over 7% of Davao del Norte's total provincial land area.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Which high-performing local barangay achieved the 'Top 1 Outstanding Barangay' honor for local governance?
questions.context_note: Barangay Dapco emerged at the top spot for outstanding local community leadership and clean management metrics.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: Which local barangay secured the 'Top 2 Outstanding Barangay' rank right alongside Dapco?
questions.context_note: Barangay Southern Davao was formally honored as the second-highest ranking outstanding barangay division.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: Which coastal barangay is widely known for hosting vast stretches of residential communities along the shoreline?
questions.context_note: Cagangohan represents a vital coastal population cluster with deep ties to seafaring trade and shoreline community life.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Which unique agricultural barangay is named directly after a visionary industrial developer and patriarch of TADECO?
questions.context_note: Barangay A.O. Floirendo is named to honor Antonio O. Floirendo Sr., who founded the massive agricultural layouts nearby.
questions.source_title: Panabo City, Davao del Norte Profile - PhilAtlas

questions.question: Panabo sits geographically wedged directly between which two major cities along the main national highway?

options.C: General Santos City and Koronadal City

questions.context_note: Panabo's geographic position makes it a classic satellite transit nexus between Davao City and provincial capital Tagum City.
questions.source_title: Discover Panabo City: Attractions & History (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What core cultural concept does the word 'Binulig' signify in the local agricultural community?

options.A: A single piece of fallen leaf
options.C: A cluster or big bunch of bananas
options.D: A rhythmic assembly of fishing boats

questions.context_note: 'Binulig' translates to a full cluster or bundle of bananas, perfectly illustrating communal unity and shared heavy harvests.
questions.source_title: Festivalscape - Binulig Festival Directory

questions.question: The historical celebrations for 'Araw ng Panabo' typically span over which calendar timeframe?
questions.context_note: Araw ng Panabo events launch dynamically around March 20, leading right up to the definitive cityhood anniversary on March 31.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What popular festive season takes place in Panabo during the month of December to honor holiday sharing?
questions.context_note: 'Paskohan sa Panabo' is a deeply cherished local tradition focused on holiday light installations, feeding programs, and music.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Which popular creative sub-culture event was spotlighted during the Silver Anniversary cityhood celebration?
questions.context_note: The DYOSA Drag Queen Competition was a massively attended cultural highlight showcasing local diversity and artistic performance styles.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: How long does the Binulig Festival celebration last?
questions.context_note: The Binulig Festival is an annual week-long celebration held every second week of October.
questions.source_title: Festivalscape - Binulig Festival Directory

questions.question: What festival did Binulig replace?
questions.context_note: The event was originally known as the Banana Festival before being rebranded to Binulig (meaning a cluster/bunch of bananas).
questions.source_title: Festivalscape - Binulig Festival Directory

questions.question: What grand, solemn mass is traditionally performed to gather leaders and citizens together at the closing of cityhood week?
questions.context_note: The Misa Pasasalamat (Thanksgiving Mass) gathers public figures and citizens alike in an open prayer of gratitude for growth.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: Which popular modern OPM musical artist headlined the massive free public concert at the conclusion of Panabo's recent milestone cityhood event?
questions.context_note: The chart-topping artist Maki drew enormous local crowds to the main public arena, concluding with a celebratory fireworks display.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: What core element of the local landscape is heavily integrated into floats, street dancing outfits, and parade themes during Binulig?

options.B: The banana plant and its bright yellow fruit

questions.context_note: Since it represents their signature export product, the banana is the center point theme of street dancing aesthetics.
questions.source_title: About Panabo City - Discover Davao

questions.question: What do the civic, multi-cultural street pageants of Araw ng Panabo primarily seek to showcase?

options.A: The historical transition from an ancient rainforest into a modern agro-industrial city
options.C: The history of Spanish fortresses in eastern Mindanao

questions.context_note: The cultural presentations celebrate multi-ethnic convergence, tracking how early tribal grounds bloomed into today's modern economic hub.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What museum in Panabo City preserves the city's cultural heritage?
questions.context_note: Museo Panabo serves as the central repository for the city's indigenous artifacts and historical documents.
questions.source_title: Davao del Norte State College Academic News Hub

questions.question: The signature banana-inspired public water fountain at Freedom Park was sculpted by which renowned Mindanao artist?
questions.context_note: Acclaimed artist Kublai Millan crafted the iconic center-park piece to proudly mirror Panabo's deep-set agricultural roots.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: What structure was retrofitted and redesigned by the city tourism office to house the galleries of Museo Panabo?

options.A: An abandoned maritime shipping container terminal
options.B: The historical Old Panabo City Hall building
options.C: The original rural bank vault house

questions.context_note: The local government adapted the old city hall property into a cultural museum space to protect regional historical facts.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: What massive indoor sports assembly complex can hold up to 10,000 spectators and hosts elite PBA basketball matchups in the city?

options.B: Panabo Multi-Purpose Tourism, Cultural and Sports Center

questions.context_note: This premiere center serves as the anchor point for massive public assemblies, major concert productions, and sports events.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: What primary feature makes Freedom Park a highly popular gathering spot for Panaboan families on weekends?

options.A: Vast commercial underground shopping arcade walkways
options.B: Lush green open spaces, weekend food markets, and cultural landmarks

questions.context_note: Freedom Park functions as the green lung of downtown Panabo, hosting regular local events and night markets.
questions.source_title: About Panabo City - Discover Davao

questions.question: What type of natural attraction in Panabo is known for its ecological importance?
questions.context_note: The Panabo Mangrove Park is highly recognized for protecting coastal biodiversity along the Davao Gulf.
questions.source_title: ResearchGate - Location and Ecosystem of Panabo Mangrove Park

questions.question: Which coastal destination in Panabo is popular for its panoramic sea views and open-air seaside dining options?
questions.context_note: The local seawall is a favorite spot for evening strolls and enjoying fresh seafood alongside views of the gulf.
questions.source_title: Panabo City: History and Attractions (Scribd Archive)

questions.question: Which prominent local state educational institution anchors academic and agricultural research excellence inside the city bounds?
questions.context_note: DNSC collaborates closely with the local government unit (LGU) on heritage documentation and coastal conservation programs.
questions.source_title: Davao del Norte State College Academic News Hub

questions.question: Which elite defense tactical units were formally recognized for outstanding peace-keeping service during recent cityhood milestones?

options.B: The 27th Infantry (Action) Battalion and local police mobile forces

questions.context_note: The 27th Infantry Battalion and the 2nd Davao del Norte Provincial Mobile Force Company were honored for ensuring community stability.
questions.source_title: A decade and a half of resilience, shared progress, and transformation - SunStar

questions.question: Jasmine Bacurnay Lee, a notable person from Panabo, is known as what in South Korea?
questions.context_note: Panabo-born Jasmine Lee achieved prominence in South Korea as both a television actress and the first naturalized citizen elected to the National Assembly.
questions.source_title: Wikipedia - Jasmine Bacurnay Lee Profile
